definition: A directed consumes edge in the provider–consumer graph: a record that one consumer depends on one provider, surfaced by WE introspection at the DI seam (Injector.consumptionEdges(), opt-in via Injector.trackConsumption). Bidirectional by construction — the same edge answers both 'what do I depend on?' and 'who depends on me?', the latter being what makes impact analysis possible. Build-derived edges are confirmed; a runtime drift agent may add potential edges until a trace confirms them.
usage: Part 1 of the Provider–Consumer Graph Protocol (#401), populated by the introspection extension in #400. The graph is build-time auto-derived (no live agent), the #092 most-permissive ingestion default.
